did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a review of computational methods for pigmented skin lesion classification in dermatology. It explicitly discusses and describes the use of AI/ML techniques including feature extraction, feature selection, pattern analysis, and classification algorithms (classifiers) used in Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems to distinguish between benign and malignant skin lesions using macroscopic and dermoscopic images.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper is explicitly labeled as a 'REVIEW' in the header and title ('Computational methods for pigmented skin lesion classification in images: review and future trends'). The abstract states, 'An overview of the main and current computational methods... is addressed in this review,' and the introduction confirms it provides a discussion on the application of such methods and future trends, summarizing prior literature rather than presenting original experimental results.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
